clean_body: | | | | | Peter, I wanted to write to you personally this evening. At midnight tonight, the Federal Election Commission will require us to close the books on our Q3 fundraising. That means our Cash on Hand number will become public — the number by which campaigns are judged by the media, outside groups, and most importantly, those working to defeat us. I ran for Congress to secure the border and restore law and order to our communities. I’m working directly with President Trump to deliver harsher penalties for illegal immigrant criminals and the most dangerous offenders on our streets. The message is simple: follow the law or face the consequences. While we have made major progress, there is work to be done. We must reverse the damage of Biden’s failed policies and ensure that career criminals — like the repeat offender who murdered an innocent woman in Charlotte — are never again put back on the street by soft-on-crime Democrats. Winning in 2026 is going to take a strong war chest. I need you in the fight, and it all starts now. | | | | Thank you for standing with me, | | | | Rep. Brad KnottNorth Carolina’s 13th Congressional District | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Brad Knott for Congress Copyright © 2025 Knott for Congress Our mailing address is: Brad Knott for Congress PO Box 97275 Raleigh, NC 27624 If you would like to donate online, please visit our website . If you have any suggestions, comments, complaints, or feedback, please send your ideas to mailto:info@bradknott.com.

name: DNC HQ
subject: Last call: Can you chip in to the DNC before tonight’s deadline?
body:
---
$5. That’s the donation we’re asking you to make to the DNC before our end-of-quarter deadline tonight. $5 because a strong fundraising report will send a powerful message to Trump and his allies: that there is serious opposition to their efforts to dismantle our democracy and silence anyone who disagrees with them. $5 because there are major elections rapidly approaching, and we only have a few weeks left to reach undecided voters in states like Virginia, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey before they head to the ballot box. $5 because the only way Democrats will win back Congress and ultimately, the White House, is if everyone steps up and does their part to help. $5 may not seem like it can do all of that, but when we combine your $5 contribution with contributions from thousands of other grassroots supporters just like you, it goes a _very_ long way. [**So, will you pitch in $5 right now to help us meet our end-of-quarter fundraising goal?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?refcode=em_20250930_4705_fr&amount=5&refcode2=4705_22930333_iuWD5P&akid=4705%2E22930333%2EiuWD5P)** _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $5](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=em_20250930_4705_fr&refcode2=4705_22930333_iuWD5P&akid=4705%2E22930333%2EiuWD5P)**

name: Marie
subject: Fighting tooth and nail
body:
| | Hi Peter, it’s Marie with one last note before we close the books on this quarter in just a few hours.

There’s a lot at stake in this race, from lowering costs for everyday families to protecting Social Security and Medicare. I’m ready to fight tooth and nail to make sure we win this, but I can’t do it alone.

It’s critical that we hit our $50,000 goal before midnight tonight to have a strong fundraising report for the quarter. Everyone’s going to be checking out how much we’ve raised to determine how and if they’re going to invest in our race.

Will you help us get across the finish line by pitching in now? Any amount helps.

[https://go.marieforcongress.com/a/september](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116300867/573142549/-1068804189?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_180DO_250930_4_16&refcode2=6ab7b7c0-6a9e-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWI3YjdjMC02YTllLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dEHvm7Hh2NTI54DV3JK2t6r7ix5m_LVuyAIim3Ad9wI=)

Thank you for all of your support,

Marie

name: PROP 50 @ California Dems
subject: We didn’t budget for a major statewide ballot initiative
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Hi everyone,
We need to be direct: There is nothing more crucial in the coming weeks than passing Proposition 50 — the Election Rigging Response Act.
Prop 50 is our firewall against Trump’s election-rigging scheme. If it fails, MAGA Republicans will stack the deck and lock in control of Congress before next year’s midterm elections even begin.
That’s why our organizers are out every single day:
➡️ Knocking doors across California to tell voters the truth about Prop 50;
➡️ Phone banking and text banking to reach voters before Trump’s lies sink in;
➡️ Mobilizing voter protection efforts, including staffing our hotline to ensure we can address questions and help every single Californian cast their vote.
➡️ Printing and distributing literature in multiple languages so every voter has the facts they need to vote YES on Prop 50;
But all of this — canvasses, phone banks, and voter protection hotlines — costs serious money. We didn’t budget for a major statewide ballot initiative this year, so we constantly rely on grassroots donors to keep our initiatives funded .
And at midnight tonight, we face our final public FEC End-of-Quarter deadline for the Proposition 50 campaign. Trump cronies and GOP dark money donors will leaf through our filings, seeing if we have the resources to out-match them or not. If they see our fundraising slowing down, you better believe that Republicans will spend MASSIVE cash to defeat Prop 50. That’s why we need your support so urgently. Will you pitch in? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-sept25-redistricting?&refcode=&amount=]

name: National Democratic Redistricting Committee
subject: Where we go after Texas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
How we got to this critical inflection point in the fight for fair maps.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116301388/573142894/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNhZTAwZDg2LTZlOWUtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=y5PnZI8CyPuMzdtyx22h_8iAn991ygMRuKPu7wajNts=&emci=c3e6bcbe-3a9e-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=cae00d86-6e9e-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=8431045)
---

**FIRST** : Donald Trump picked up the phone and demanded that Texas Governor Greg Abbott find him five congressional seats. **THEN** : Texas Republicans drew up an egregiously gerrymandered map behind closed doors. Texas Democrats left the state to stop it, and Republicans called for their arrest. Republicans forced through their map despite massive outcry. **NOW** : The fair maps movement is taking Texas to court over its racial gerrymander that dilutes the voting power of communities of color. But Republicans aren’t done trying to cheat their way through the midterms. Trump and his allies are demanding _more_ seats in _more_ states — from Indiana to Ohio and Florida. **Peter, this fight is happening on all fronts and it takes enormous resources for us to hold the line.** The last few months have been nonstop attacks on democracy, and there are new threats of extreme gerrymandering still on the horizon. We’re at a critical inflection point in the fight for fair maps, and we can’t afford to let up. [**Donate now to help us give this fight everything we’ve got to preserve our democracy. How we respond in this moment will have ripple effects for years to come — so please don’t hesitate to do your part. Rush your gift to support the work of the NDRC today.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116301389/573142895/1110433579?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TXFTNReduxNOR_email_20250930-NDRC-Tier1-TXFTNReduxNOR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250930-NDRC-Tier1-TXFTNReduxNOR_&utm_campaign=TXFTNReduxNOR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNhZTAwZDg2LTZlOWUtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=y5PnZI8CyPuMzdtyx22h_8iAn991ygMRuKPu7wajNts=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: How we got to this critical inflection point in the fight for fair maps. | FIRST : Donald Trump picked up the phone and demanded that Texas Governor Greg Abbott find him five congressional seats. THEN : Texas Republicans drew up an egregiously gerrymandered map behind closed doors. Texas Democrats left the state to stop it, and Republicans called for their arrest. Republicans forced through their map despite massive outcry. NOW : The fair maps movement is taking Texas to court over its racial gerrymander that dilutes the voting power of communities of color. But Republicans aren’t done trying to cheat their way through the midterms. Trump and his allies are demanding more seats in more states — from Indiana to Ohio and Florida. Peter, this fight is happening on all fronts and it takes enormous resources for us to hold the line. The last few months have been nonstop attacks on democracy, and there are new threats of extreme gerrymandering still on the horizon. We’re at a critical inflection point in the fight for fair maps, and we can’t afford to let up. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you, National Democratic Redistricting Committee = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. name: Hakeem Jeffries
subject: Donald Trump is unserious
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeffries for Congress
body:

name: Congressman Al Green, TX-09
subject: Midnight Deadline: My voice is on the line
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/925txftbackemtx [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/925txftbackemtx]
Friend,
In just a few hours, our midnight fundraising deadline will pass, and with it, a critical opportunity to fortify our movement.
My commitment has always been simple: to fight for justice for all people, whether they are in Houston's Third Ward or in Gaza.
It is precisely because I use my voice to speak for the voiceless—because I stand up to powerful interests and demand human rights for all—that those same interests have placed a target on my back. Their new redistricting maps are a political weapon designed to remove me from Congress and silence our cause.
Their entire strategy is built on one cynical belief: that we won't have the resources to fight back when it counts.
Tonight, before 11:59 PM, I am asking you to help me prove them wrong.
Losing this seat is not an option. It means one less voice for peace in the halls of Congress. One less vote for human rights. One less fighter standing up to the bullies.
We must show them the strength of our convictions tonight. Your contribution right now sends a powerful message that we are ready for the fight ahead.
Please, make an emergency contribution before the midnight deadline to help me continue serving you and fighting for justice for all.
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/925txftbackemtx]

In the fight,
Congressman Al Green

Peter, It’s official - Trump and his MAGA nuts just shut down the government. name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: Shutdown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
The U.S. government shut down at 12:01 AM.
This is not a fight over tax credits. This is a fight over your ability to go to a pharmacy and afford your insulin.
We’re not talking about just 15% or 20% increases in people’s premiums.
Healthcare companies are finalizing the premiums that everyday Americans are going to be paying. And we are talking about a doubling of monthly healthcare premiums not even including deductibles, in terms of what people are going to be paying next year, because of a trillion dollars in Medicaid cuts that were put together earlier this year.
I don’t know about anybody else, but I do not think this is just about a tax break or small tax extender. This is about fighting for community health centers, and ensuring that we have stability of the American healthcare system.
**And when Republicans use a legislative mechanism to gut the American healthcare system, we have to use a legislative mechanism in order to restore it and fight for it.**
In solidarity,
Alexandria
Our movement for real progress will have to push harder than ever these next four years.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251001-1.DpG_Hf&refcode=em20251001-9864-P_FND_RES_CTR&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9864%2E2526796%2Ev0WTSk)

name: Office of U.S. Senator Angela Alsobrooks
subject: Senator Alsobrooks Newsletter -- Government Shutdown
body:
|

---
Dear friends and neighbors, As you are now aware, the federal government shut down at midnight - and make no mistake about who's responsible for this. The Republicans have the keys to the kingdom. They control the White House. They have majorities in the Senate and House. They decided to shut down this government. This has been their plan from the beginning. Republicans have said they want our patriotic federal workers to be traumatized. They have a particular disdain for our civil servants - the very people who make sure our grandparents get their social security, who make sure our veterans get the care they need, who make sure our food and water are safe. All of this - the economic downturn, the cruel and callous firing of civil servants, the attacks on the middle class, the shutdown of government operations - all of it is a reflection of Republican leadership, or lack thereof. I will fight, alongside my colleagues, to reopen this government and put a stop to the cruelty Republicans continue to inflict upon Marylanders. And I will continue to work to minimize the impact on those who can least afford it. **The Help FEDS Act** This week I, along with Congresswoman Sarah Elfreth, introduced legislation to protect essential federal workers - the _Help FEDS (Federal Employees During a Shutdown) Act_. This legislation would support federal employees who would be required to work without pay during a government shutdown. President Trump, Elon Musk, and Russell Vought have shown us very clearly over the last nine months that they do not care about our civil servants - the patriotic men and women that work on behalf of the American people under Democratic and Republican presidents alike. "Federal employees, through no fault of their own, are caught in the middle of a political dispute that could force them to go to work without knowing when they will be paid. The Help FEDS Act would provide these nonpartisan civil servants with vital resources to help keep gas in their cars, provide food and necessary medicines for their family and give them peace of mind as they navigate a period of immense financial uncertainty while delivering essential services Americans depend on. I applaud Senator Alsobrooks and Rep. Elfreth for their efforts to help ease the burden placed on these dedicated public servants and for their leadership on this issue," said **Doreen Greenwald, NTEU National President**. "In the event of a shutdown, excepted federal workers will be required to work without pay. The least we can do is to allow them to collect unemployment benefits," said **Andrew Stettner, Director of Economy & Jobs, the Century Foundation**. "Federal workers should not be pawns in government shutdowns, and if they are required to work without pay, then they should at least receive unemployment insurance. We appreciate Senator Alsobrooks stepping forward as their champion and introducing this important legislation," said **Judy Conti, Government Affairs Director, National Employment Law Project**. **This legislation has been endorsed by** the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, the National Treasury Employees Union, the Century Foundation, and the National Employment Law Project. When there is a government shutdown, federal employees who are deemed "excepted" or "essential" are required to work without pay. The President and OMB Director have wide discretion over how many federal employees may be deemed essential. During the last government shutdown, when the Trump Administration was in charge, 420,000 federal employees were deemed essential, meaning that 420,000 were forced to work without pay. _The Help FEDS Act_ ensures federal employees who are required to work during a government shutdown are eligible to apply for unemployment insurance (UI). Essential employees will receive back pay thanks to legislation that Congress passed after the 2018/2019 shutdown. Under this bill, employees would have to pay back the UI when they receive back pay, and states will be reimbursed for the additional UI they would have to pay out. Furloughed employees during a shutdown are already eligible for UI. My legislation closes a loophole and puts excepted employees in parity with furloughed employees. Read more in the Baltimore Sun:[ Alsobrooks, Elfreth to introduce bill to compensate shutdown workers ](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.baltimoresun.com%2f2025%2f09%2f29%2falsobrooks-elfreth-bill-compensate-shutdown-workers%2f&redir_log=405877740215641) Read the full bill text [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.alsobrooks.senate.gov%2fwp-content%2fuploads%2f2025%2f09%2fGOE25778-1.pdf&redir_log=112434474835478). **Federal Government Shutdown Resources and FAQs** My office will remain open during the shutdown to answer questions and provide guidance on federal matters. Over 269,000 federal employees living in Maryland will be impacted by this Republican government shutdown. _Our website, [alsobrooks.senate.gov/shutdown](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.alsobrooks.senate.gov%2fshutdown%2f&redir_log=533032475571306) will be updated as we learn more._ **RESOURCES**

Federal employees should monitor [www.opm.gov](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.opm.gov%2f&redir_log=547825654133138) for updates. Please click [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.opm.gov%2fpolicy-data-oversight%2fpay-leave%2ffurlough-guidance%2f%23url%3dOverview&redir_log=183304614575585) to find more guidance from OPM. Plan](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.ssa.gov%2fagency%2fshutdown%2fmaterials%2fcontingency-plan-09-24-25.pdf&redir_log=134673819551503) [U.S. Department of Commerce Contingency Plan](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.commerce.gov%2ffurlough-information&redir_log=877669283094324) [U.S. Department of State Contingency Plan](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.state.gov%2fwp-content%2fuploads%2f2025%2f09%2fDOS-Lapse-Guidance-updated-29-September-2025.pdf&redir_log=4436345818636) [NASA Contingency Plan](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.nasa.gov%2fwp-content%2fuploads%2f2025%2f09%2fnasa-continuity-of-appropriations-plan-final-9-29-2025.pdf%3femrc%3dcd4554&redir_log=06273877969777) **FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS**
_This information will continue to be updated_. WHAT IS A GOVERNMENT SHUTDOWN?
* According to the [Congressional Research Service](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.congress.gov%2fcrs-product%2fR41759&redir_log=004355365006287), when Federal government agencies and programs lack budget authority after the expiration of either full-year or interim appropriations, they experience a "funding gap."
* Under the Antideficiency Act (31 U.S.C. §§1341 et seq.), they must cease operations, except in certain circumstances when continued activities are authorized by law. When there is a funding gap that affects many federal entities, the situation is often referred to as a government shutdown.
* Due to the lapse in funding, many Federal agencies may have to furlough workers and would have limited capacity to work on cases.
* However, my office remains open and ready to assist you.
* Due to the shutdown, depending on decisions from the Trump Administration, some Federal agencies may not begin working on your case immediately, but our office will do our best to get you the assistance you need in a timely manner.

WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY SOCIAL SECURITY BENEFITS?
* Yes, according to the Social Security Administration, recipients will continue to receive Social Security benefits and SSI checks.

WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY MEDICARE AND/OR MEDICAID BENEFITS?
* Yes, Medicare and Medicaid recipients will still receive benefits.
* However, you will not be able to get a new or replacement Medicare card, and benefit verifications will stop.

WILL THE CHILDREN'S HEALTH INSURANCE PROGRAM (CHIP) BE AFFECTED?
* No. According to CMS's FY26 contingency plan, CMS will maintain the staff necessary to make payments for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY VETERANS' BENEFITS? _Per VA guidance, the following will not be impacted by the shutdown:_
* VA Medical Centers, Outpatient Clinics, and Vet Centers will be open as usual and providing all services.
* VA benefits will continue to be processed and delivered, including compensation, pension, education, and housing benefits.
* Burials will continue at VA national cemeteries. Applications for headstones, markers, and burial benefits processing will continue.
* The Board of Veterans' Appeals will continue decisions on Veterans' cases.
* Call Centers: The VA's primary call center (1-800-MyVA411) and the Veterans Crisis Line (Dial 988, Press 1) are open 24/7.

_Per VA guidance, the following services will be impacted:_
* VA will not provide Veteran career counseling or transition assistance program activities.
* Call Centers: The GI Bill Hotline (1-888-GIBILL-1) will be closed and National Cemetery Applicant Assistance (1-800-697-6947) hotline will be closed.
* VA benefits regional offices will be closed.
* VA will cease public affairs and outreach to Veterans, including social media, VetResources emails, and responses to press inquiries.
* VA will not permanently place headstones or maintain the grounds at VA national cemeteries.
* VA will not process applications for pre-need burials at VA cemeteries.
* VA will not print new presidential memorial certificates.
* VA will cease outreach to state, county, tribal, municipal, faith-based, and community-based partners by the VA Central Office.

_For more information, please[visit the VA's website here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fdepartment.va.gov%2fcontingency-planning%2f&redir_log=258908356363333)._ WILL ACTIVE-DUTY SERVICEMEMBERS BE PAID?
* According to Department of Defense guidance, active duty servicemembers do not receive their paychecks during a shutdown but will continue working as they are deemed "essential."
* Servicemembers will receive back pay, but paycheck deposits will be delayed.
* If you live in base housing, your services will remain intact. Schools operated by the Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) will remain open.
* On-base Child Development Centers will close on a case-by-case basis. Please check locally for closures.
* Civilian personnel who are necessary to carry out expected activities will continue to work, but also will not be paid, and some civilian personnel will be furloughed without pay.
* USAA offers [interest-free loans](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.usaa.com%2finet%2fwc%2fent-gov-shutdown-assistance-landing-mkt&redir_log=75513517742480) and Navy Federal Credit Union offers [paycheck assistance](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.navyfederal.org%2fabout%2fpress-releases%2f2024%2fnavy-federal-offers-paycheck-relief-to-members-impacted-by-government-shutdown.html&redir_log=397786780427962) to servicemembers and DoD civilians during a shutdown.

WHAT HAPPENS TO SNAP/WIC BENEFITS?
* USDA will continue to administer the SNAP program.
* WIC operations will continue, though the program is low on funds and participants could experience disruptions if a shutdown lasts longer than a week, according to the National WIC Association.

WILL HEAD START PROGRAMS CLOSE?
* Most Head Start programs are not expected to be impacted by the Federal shutdown.

WHAT HAPPENS TO FEDERAL LOANS DURING A SHUTDOWN?
* The Pell Grant and Federal Direct Student Loan programs are considered mandatory, meaning the programs can still make payments during a shutdown.
* However, staff may be furloughed, so assistance may be delayed.

WHAT HAPPENS TO PROGRAMS WITHIN THE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE?
* According to USDA, the Department will continue operations related to: certain farm loan processing items, certain natural resource and conservation programs, core nutrition safety net programs, essential food safety operations (inspections and laboratory work) to protect public health, responding to and preparing for wildland fires, activities supported by user fees (including grading, assessment, inspection, import and export), animal and plant health emergency programs (including new world screwworm, highly pathogenic avian influenza, exotic fruit flies, African swine fever, and rabies), Section 521 Rental Assistance Program outlays of prior obligations, human resource policies and systems, critical IT infrastructure and cybersecurity, emergency operations, and national defense and homeland security readiness.
* According to USDA, the Department will cease operations related to: payment processing, disaster assistance processing, technical assistance, contracts and agreements not related to exempted programs, financial management beyond funds management, regulatory work, travel, trainings, trade negotiations, timber sales, hazardous fuels treatments, special use permits, surveys for high-risk plant pests and diseases for certain swine, cattle, and aquatic animal diseases, long-term studies and research on animal diseases, research and report publications, website updates, data products, and the majority of Risk Management Agency, Natural Resources Conservation Service, Foreign Agricultural Service, Food and Nutrition Service, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Economic Research Service, National Agricultural Statistical Service, Rural Development, and Staff Office activities.
* For more information about specific agencies, the USDA plan can be [viewed here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.usda.gov%2fsites%2fdefault%2ffiles%2fdocuments%2ffy2026-usda-lapse-plan.pdf&redir_log=453019633994529).

HOW IS THE SMALL BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION (SBA) IMPACTED?
* The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will pause most new business loan approvals and program support during a government shutdown -- including 7(a) and 504 loans.
* However, SBA's disaster loan processing is expected to continue.
* The SBA has published its "Plan for Operating in the Event of a Lapse in Appropriations - September 2025." For more information, please [visit the SBA's website here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.sba.gov%2fsba-employee-information-shutdown-furlough&redir_log=244887925198446).

WILL TSA SECURITY LINES BE LONGER AT THE AIRPORT?
* Most TSA airport personnel and FAA air traffic controllers would be required to continue working, without pay, during a shutdown.
* However, airports may experience longer-than-usual security lines, so please plan to arrive early for any travel.

CAN I STILL GET A NEW PASSPORT DURING A SHUTDOWN?
* Yes, passport applications will continue to be processed, but please note, response times may be longer than usual.

CAN I STILL GET A GUIDED TOUR OF THE U.S. CAPITOL OR WHITE HOUSE?
* Unfortunately, due to the federal shutdown, all federal buildings will be closed for the duration of the shutdown. If you have a tour booked of the Capitol, Supreme Court, Library of Congress, or Bureau of Engraving and Printing, your tours will be canceled and not automatically rescheduled unless you request to do so. Please contact my office at [tours@alsobrooks.senate.gov](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.alsobrooks.senate.gov%2fservices%2ftour-requests%2f&redir_log=878974634037406) or at 202-224-4524 with any questions.

WILL NATIONAL PARKS REMAIN OPEN?
* National Park closures will be determined on a location-by-location basis; open-air parks and monuments in Washington, D.C. will likely remain accessible. Smithsonian museums may also be impacted by a lengthy shutdown.
* Many services and facilities would be affected - including visitor centers, educational programs, restrooms, trash collection, and road maintenance.

TOURING WASHINGTON, DC
* Unfortunately, due to the federal shutdown, all federal buildings will be closed for the duration of the shutdown. If you have a tour booked of the Capitol, Supreme Court, Library of Congress, or Bureau of Engraving and Printing, your tours will be canceled and not automatically rescheduled unless you request to do so. Please contact my office at tours@alsobrooks.senate.gov or at 202-224-4524 with any questions.

**Still Working For You** I know these are difficult times. But please know that I will continue fighting tooth and nail to reopen this government, and for our federal workers and the American people they serve. My office will remain open and operational to support constituents. You can visit [alsobrooks.senate.gov/shutdown](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2falsobrooks.senate.gov%2fshutdown&redir_log=66116639934267) or call one of our offices:
* DC: 202-224-4524
* Bowie: 301-860-0414
* Rockville: 240-599-7390
* Easton: 410-546-4250
* Baltimore: 410-962-4436

**Share Your Story** We know government shutdowns can be very difficult for Marylanders, particularly for civil servants and their families. We know that Republicans are going to try and use this shutdown to fire civil servants and raise health care costs. I am committed to fighting for our civil servants and for every middle and working class Marylander. As I continue to meet with Marylanders, I also want to hear directly from you about how this shutdown is affecting you, your family, or someone you love. I'm going to keep sharing your stories. [Tell us your story here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15482.12049215.5672426.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fforms.gle%2f1Fm2HT3XVwtDgUu18&redir_log=648644667522229). We will not give up. I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. You can call the office, connect with us, or follow us on Bluesky, Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube, and X. We are in this TOGETHER. Sincerely, Angela D. Alsobrooks
United States Senator
clean_body: | Dear friends and neighbors, As you are now aware, the federal government shut down at midnight - and make no mistake about who's responsible for this. The Republicans have the keys to the kingdom. They control the White House. They have majorities in the Senate and House. They decided to shut down this government. This has been their plan from the beginning. Republicans have said they want our patriotic federal workers to be traumatized. They have a particular disdain for our civil servants - the very people who make sure our grandparents get their social security, who make sure our veterans get the care they need, who make sure our food and water are safe. All of this - the economic downturn, the cruel and callous firing of civil servants, the attacks on the middle class, the shutdown of government operations - all of it is a reflection of Republican leadership, or lack thereof. I will fight, alongside my colleagues, to reopen this government and put a stop to the cruelty Republicans continue to inflict upon Marylanders. And I will continue to work to minimize the impact on those who can least afford it. The Help FEDS Act This week I, along with Congresswoman Sarah Elfreth, introduced legislation to protect essential federal workers - the Help FEDS (Federal Employees During a Shutdown) Act. This legislation would support federal employees who would be required to work without pay during a government shutdown. President Trump, Elon Musk, and Russell Vought have shown us very clearly over the last nine months that they do not care about our civil servants - the patriotic men and women that work on behalf of the American people under Democratic and Republican presidents alike. "Federal employees, through no fault of their own, are caught in the middle of a political dispute that could force them to go to work without knowing when they will be paid. The Help FEDS Act would provide these nonpartisan civil servants with vital resources to help keep gas in their cars, provide food and necessary medicines for their family and give them peace of mind as they navigate a period of immense financial uncertainty while delivering essential services Americans depend on. I applaud Senator Alsobrooks and Rep. Elfreth for their efforts to help ease the burden placed on these dedicated public servants and for their leadership on this issue," said Doreen Greenwald, NTEU National President. "In the event of a shutdown, excepted federal workers will be required to work without pay. The least we can do is to allow them to collect unemployment benefits," said Andrew Stettner, Director of Economy & Jobs, the Century Foundation. "Federal workers should not be pawns in government shutdowns, and if they are required to work without pay, then they should at least receive unemployment insurance. We appreciate Senator Alsobrooks stepping forward as their champion and introducing this important legislation," said Judy Conti, Government Affairs Director, National Employment Law Project. This legislation has been endorsed by the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, the National Treasury Employees Union, the Century Foundation, and the National Employment Law Project. When there is a government shutdown, federal employees who are deemed "excepted" or "essential" are required to work without pay. The President and OMB Director have wide discretion over how many federal employees may be deemed essential. During the last government shutdown, when the Trump Administration was in charge, 420,000 federal employees were deemed essential, meaning that 420,000 were forced to work without pay. The Help FEDS Act ensures federal employees who are required to work during a government shutdown are eligible to apply for unemployment insurance (UI). Essential employees will receive back pay thanks to legislation that Congress passed after the 2018/2019 shutdown. Under this bill, employees would have to pay back the UI when they receive back pay, and states will be reimbursed for the additional UI they would have to pay out. Furloughed employees during a shutdown are already eligible for UI. My legislation closes a loophole and puts excepted employees in parity with furloughed employees. Read more in the Baltimore Sun: Read the full bill text . Federal Government Shutdown Resources and FAQs My office will remain open during the shutdown to answer questions and provide guidance on federal matters. Over 269,000 federal employees living in Maryland will be impacted by this Republican government shutdown. Our website, will be updated as we learn more. RESOURCES Federal employees should monitor for updates. Please click to find more guidance from OPM. FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS This information will continue to be updated. WHAT IS A GOVERNMENT SHUTDOWN? According to the , when Federal government agencies and programs lack budget authority after the expiration of either full-year or interim appropriations, they experience a "funding gap." Under the Antideficiency Act (31 U.S.C. §§1341 et seq.), they must cease operations, except in certain circumstances when continued activities are authorized by law. When there is a funding gap that affects many federal entities, the situation is often referred to as a government shutdown. Due to the lapse in funding, many Federal agencies may have to furlough workers and would have limited capacity to work on cases. However, my office remains open and ready to assist you. Due to the shutdown, depending on decisions from the Trump Administration, some Federal agencies may not begin working on your case immediately, but our office will do our best to get you the assistance you need in a timely manner. WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY SOCIAL SECURITY BENEFITS? Yes, according to the Social Security Administration, recipients will continue to receive Social Security benefits and SSI checks. WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY MEDICARE AND/OR MEDICAID BENEFITS? Yes, Medicare and Medicaid recipients will still receive benefits. However, you will not be able to get a new or replacement Medicare card, and benefit verifications will stop. WILL THE CHILDREN'S HEALTH INSURANCE PROGRAM (CHIP) BE AFFECTED? No. According to CMS's FY26 contingency plan, CMS will maintain the staff necessary to make payments for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP). WILL I STILL RECEIVE MY VETERANS' BENEFITS? Per VA guidance, the following will not be impacted by the shutdown: VA Medical Centers, Outpatient Clinics, and Vet Centers will be open as usual and providing all services. VA benefits will continue to be processed and delivered, including compensation, pension, education, and housing benefits. Burials will continue at VA national cemeteries. Applications for headstones, markers, and burial benefits processing will continue. The Board of Veterans' Appeals will continue decisions on Veterans' cases. Call Centers: The VA's primary call center (1-800-MyVA411) and the Veterans Crisis Line (Dial 988, Press 1) are open 24/7. Per VA guidance, the following services will be impacted: VA will not provide Veteran career counseling or transition assistance program activities. Call Centers: The GI Bill Hotline (1-888-GIBILL-1) will be closed and National Cemetery Applicant Assistance (1-800-697-6947) hotline will be closed. VA benefits regional offices will be closed. VA will cease public affairs and outreach to Veterans, including social media, VetResources emails, and responses to press inquiries. VA will not permanently place headstones or maintain the grounds at VA national cemeteries. VA will not process applications for pre-need burials at VA cemeteries. VA will not print new presidential memorial certificates. VA will cease outreach to state, county, tribal, municipal, faith-based, and community-based partners by the VA Central Office. For more information, please. WILL ACTIVE-DUTY SERVICEMEMBERS BE PAID? According to Department of Defense guidance, active duty servicemembers do not receive their paychecks during a shutdown but will continue working as they are deemed "essential." Servicemembers will receive back pay, but paycheck deposits will be delayed. If you live in base housing, your services will remain intact. Schools operated by the Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) will remain open. On-base Child Development Centers will close on a case-by-case basis. Please check locally for closures. Civilian personnel who are necessary to carry out expected activities will continue to work, but also will not be paid, and some civilian personnel will be furloughed without pay. USAA offers and Navy Federal Credit Union offers to servicemembers and DoD civilians during a shutdown. WHAT HAPPENS TO SNAP/WIC BENEFITS? USDA will continue to administer the SNAP program. WIC operations will continue, though the program is low on funds and participants could experience disruptions if a shutdown lasts longer than a week, according to the National WIC Association. WILL HEAD START PROGRAMS CLOSE? Most Head Start programs are not expected to be impacted by the Federal shutdown. WHAT HAPPENS TO FEDERAL LOANS DURING A SHUTDOWN? The Pell Grant and Federal Direct Student Loan programs are considered mandatory, meaning the programs can still make payments during a shutdown. However, staff may be furloughed, so assistance may be delayed. WHAT HAPPENS TO PROGRAMS WITHIN THE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE? According to USDA, the Department will continue operations related to: certain farm loan processing items, certain natural resource and conservation programs, core nutrition safety net programs, essential food safety operations (inspections and laboratory work) to protect public health, responding to and preparing for wildland fires, activities supported by user fees (including grading, assessment, inspection, import and export), animal and plant health emergency programs (including new world screwworm, highly pathogenic avian influenza, exotic fruit flies, African swine fever, and rabies), Section 521 Rental Assistance Program outlays of prior obligations, human resource policies and systems, critical IT infrastructure and cybersecurity, emergency operations, and national defense and homeland security readiness. According to USDA, the Department will cease operations related to: payment processing, disaster assistance processing, technical assistance, contracts and agreements not related to exempted programs, financial management beyond funds management, regulatory work, travel, trainings, trade negotiations, timber sales, hazardous fuels treatments, special use permits, surveys for high-risk plant pests and diseases for certain swine, cattle, and aquatic animal diseases, long-term studies and research on animal diseases, research and report publications, website updates, data products, and the majority of Risk Management Agency, Natural Resources Conservation Service, Foreign Agricultural Service, Food and Nutrition Service, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Economic Research Service, National Agricultural Statistical Service, Rural Development, and Staff Office activities. For more information about specific agencies, the USDA plan can be . HOW IS THE SMALL BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION (SBA) IMPACTED? The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will pause most new business loan approvals and program support during a government shutdown -- including 7(a) and 504 loans. However, SBA's disaster loan processing is expected to continue. The SBA has published its "Plan for Operating in the Event of a Lapse in Appropriations - September 2025." For more information, please . WILL TSA SECURITY LINES BE LONGER AT THE AIRPORT? Most TSA airport personnel and FAA air traffic controllers would be required to continue working, without pay, during a shutdown. However, airports may experience longer-than-usual security lines, so please plan to arrive early for any travel. CAN I STILL GET A NEW PASSPORT DURING A SHUTDOWN? Yes, passport applications will continue to be processed, but please note, response times may be longer than usual. CAN I STILL GET A GUIDED TOUR OF THE U.S. CAPITOL OR WHITE HOUSE? Unfortunately, due to the federal shutdown, all federal buildings will be closed for the duration of the shutdown. If you have a tour booked of the Capitol, Supreme Court, Library of Congress, or Bureau of Engraving and Printing, your tours will be canceled and not automatically rescheduled unless you request to do so. Please contact my office at or at 202-224-4524 with any questions. WILL NATIONAL PARKS REMAIN OPEN? National Park closures will be determined on a location-by-location basis; open-air parks and monuments in Washington, D.C. will likely remain accessible. Smithsonian museums may also be impacted by a lengthy shutdown. Many services and facilities would be affected - including visitor centers, educational programs, restrooms, trash collection, and road maintenance. TOURING WASHINGTON, DC Unfortunately, due to the federal shutdown, all federal buildings will be closed for the duration of the shutdown. If you have a tour booked of the Capitol, Supreme Court, Library of Congress, or Bureau of Engraving and Printing, your tours will be canceled and not automatically rescheduled unless you request to do so. Please contact my office at tours@alsobrooks.senate.gov or at 202-224-4524 with any questions. Still Working For You I know these are difficult times. But please know that I will continue fighting tooth and nail to reopen this government, and for our federal workers and the American people they serve. My office will remain open and operational to support constituents. You can visit or call one of our offices: DC: 202-224-4524 Bowie: 301-860-0414 Rockville: 240-599-7390 Easton: 410-546-4250 Baltimore: 410-962-4436 Share Your Story** We know government shutdowns can be very difficult for Marylanders, particularly for civil servants and their families. We know that Republicans are going to try and use this shutdown to fire civil servants and raise health care costs. I am committed to fighting for our civil servants and for every middle and working class Marylander. As I continue to meet with Marylanders, I also want to hear directly from you about how this shutdown is affecting you, your family, or someone you love. I'm going to keep sharing your stories. . We will not give up. I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. You can call the office, connect with us, or follow us on Bluesky, Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube, and X. We are in this TOGETHER. Sincerely, Angela D. Alsobrooks United States Senator

body:
Please Join Us! No images? [Click here](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-e-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-h/) Dear ,I wanted to reach out personally to remind you about our Party's Annual Fall Dinner on **Monday, October 27, 2025, in Indianapolis**.We are excited to give **RNC Chairman Joe Gruters** a warm Hoosier welcome, alongside Republican leaders from across the state!From his start as a grassroots activist to leading the Republican National Committee, Chairman Gruters knows what it takes to win. He's helped shape victories in Florida and across the country, and now he's bringing that experience to Indiana!Fall Dinner isn't just a chance to hear from national and state leaders; it's a chance to gather as one Party. Together, we'll celebrate our shared values, hear what's ahead for our movement, and recommit ourselves to keeping Indiana strong and leading the way for the country!Grassroots Republicans like you are the heart of our Party. Whether it's knocking on doors, making calls, or showing up when it matters most, our movement is built on your energy and commitment. This dinner is an opportunity to connect, be inspired, and get ready for the important work still to come.Registration is still open, but we're selling out fast! If you have not yet secured your spot, please contact our team at **rsvp@indiana.gop**.Yours in service, **Lana Keesling**
Indiana Republican Party [](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-r/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-y/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-j/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-t/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-i/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-qdujhkk-hhlljjcji-d/) ** PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE. ****NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. ****WWW.INDIANA.GOP.** | Indiana Republican State Committee 101 W. | MassGOP Statement on Entrance of Michael Minogue Into 2026 Race for Governor
---
---
| BOSTON, MA -- Massachusetts Republican Party Chair Amy Carnevale responded to Monday's announcement by Republican Michael Minogue that he is entering the 2026 gubernatorial race.
"We are thrilled to welcome Michael Minogue into the race for governor. His entrance gives Massachusetts voters yet another alternative to the failing policies and faltering economy of the Healey administration and one-party rule. Republicans are positioned to deliver what matters most to voters: a state government they can trust, one that priorities their families and not those in power."
###

name: With Honor Action
subject: The U.S. Federal Government is Shut Down
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/c53a3a2ba05e/bats-brews-and-a-birthday-salute-to-the-us-army-14054389?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | Friends,

Government shutdowns are not acts of fate; they are the result of deep political polarization. And when Washington grinds to a halt, it’s our troops, their families, our veterans, and countless public servants who bear the burden.

Shutdowns stall military training and readiness, leave families worried about missed paychecks, and block veterans from accessing the benefits they have earned. They also weaken America’s ability to respond to threats, halt critical health research, and project instability to both allies and adversaries. A few notes on what we can expect in the coming hours and days:
* Over 330,000 Department of Defense civilians will be furloughed ➡️ [DoD Shutdown Plan](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d1ce3ce6cd&e=33f53a5b1a)
* The vast majority of the 61,000 T.S.A. workers and more than 13,000 air traffic controllers would work without pay ➡️ [DoT Shutdown Plan](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f74e141272&e=33f53a5b1a)
* The VA will stop providing transition program assistance and career counseling, and the GI Bill hotline will be closed ➡️ [VA Shutdown Information](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f905a44adb&e=33f53a5b1a)
* While most national parks will remain open, the National Park Service plans to furlough nearly two-thirds of all NPS employees ➡️ [NPS Contingency Plan ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=62fcf8b2e2&e=33f53a5b1a)

The economic cost of a government shutdown is not merely hypothetical. The Congressional Budget Office [estimated](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f2236614db&e=33f53a5b1a) that the 2018-2019 shutdown reduced Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by a total of $11 billion, including $3 billion that will never be recovered.

At With Honor, we believe America is strongest when we rise above division and put service before politics, and we are weakest when we are divided. Shutdowns don’t just signal dysfunction; they erode trust at home and abroad. We need leaders in Congress who bridge divides and keep faith with those who defend us.

Bipartisanship is not optional; it’s essential to our national security and to America’s leadership in the world.

We urge Congress to fund the government and ensure that our service members, veterans, and their families receive the support they deserve.

In Service,

Rye Barcott
Co-Founder & CEO
With Honor
---
|
---

and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | Friends, Government shutdowns are not acts of fate; they are the result of deep political polarization. And when Washington grinds to a halt, it’s our troops, their families, our veterans, and countless public servants who bear the burden. Shutdowns stall military training and readiness, leave families worried about missed paychecks, and block veterans from accessing the benefits they have earned. They also weaken America’s ability to respond to threats, halt critical health research, and project instability to both allies and adversaries. A few notes on what we can expect in the coming hours and days: Over 330,000 Department of Defense civilians will be furloughed ➡️ The vast majority of the 61,000 T.S.A. workers and more than 13,000 air traffic controllers would work without pay ➡️ The VA will stop providing transition program assistance and career counseling, and the GI Bill hotline will be closed ➡️ While most national parks will remain open, the National Park Service plans to furlough nearly two-thirds of all NPS employees ➡️ The economic cost of a government shutdown is not merely hypothetical. The Congressional Budget Office that the 2018-2019 shutdown reduced Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by a total of $11 billion, including $3 billion that will never be recovered. At With Honor, we believe America is strongest when we rise above division and put service before politics, and we are weakest when we are divided. Shutdowns don’t just signal dysfunction; they erode trust at home and abroad. We need leaders in Congress who bridge divides and keep faith with those who defend us. Bipartisanship is not optional; it’s essential to our national security and to America’s leadership in the world. We urge Congress to fund the government and ensure that our service members, veterans, and their families receive the support they deserve. In Service, Rye Barcott Co-Founder & CEO With Honor | | | | | Please follow With Honor Action on Twitter via this , and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country. | | | | | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC | | With Honor Action is a 501(c)(4), which serves as the organization’s policy and social-welfare arm. ## Federal Government Shuts Down
The [federal government shut down](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGO8yAQBeDT4I4IBoiTguJv3OVvt7SwGWxWGCwYJ8rtV94t9gDbjTTS0_vex2N8uJhiXv67DS2FMmZ8tYREWMelPLHmDTO19SBfXnmLKZKrIwgwXAou5OhdTO9Rd94GREDVoZW9uSup4Gq61fZ6MkZ5KcHcJ3kT9zAbr9Bh8NOs-msX7ZkmhZBSiF6ri_ECjDZBaa9u4DTTAn2kUi-fJWZaMaT32ewyl61LdiXaG1P_GAwMhvzeS6PzxWA4gxkMUjAYhGQwHI2fvp_rl8dPX-OnkLtAWHnD7Ai5x63M1VHjwcXEqfB27HupxBuVfXE7D0f2MS98iikxGLpq_f6KKcV27ZkWy-Zi-i5Kf1r3aeErAAD___RrkAE) last night just after midnight as Democrats rejected a resolution to fund the government for seven more weeks because it didn't include money for health-care subsidies. The White House has said it would institute widespread layoffs and potentially take [other "irreversible" actions](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ8GOpA9tuGmcLEN3d12SzTBQ5iVfyJ7EpS3X7GvcLsjHenoO9-f7_UbOXJ-_MZEXvayZjpbJBGq66O8qeZEWdrxklDOnDiyYF1BgRu0GpReA3L8rLYPficCMj15Pbub0QYm1x9-tziFO2qDMO-abmYKd3eb9c1MCib86tlfa1oprZWarRldUOCs240N5gvQdlZRYCl1_Fs4y0F7_Fxk41ZSH_0h8myd-dXB0sFynuf4waOUq-1gue50sGAV3iJdUeorPYcTa25DoFS2itIGrpXeVBvfIw24CZfcBj1b7QC0Gw9Jsa8-PE-Okds0d1Y9EnL8DyE_Mvf28C8AAP__TpWDhw) on government programs if the government shut down. President Trump can close functions he considers non-essential at agencies lacking funding. The military, federal law enforcement, the U.S. Post Office, and air traffic control will remain operational, and benefits such as Social Security and Medicare will also continue, though there may be disruptions and delays. In previous shutdowns, some federal services, including national parks, federal buildings and museums, were closed, but officials say parks may stay open this time. [Here's a list of what's expected](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAQhp8GNkdwQOIMDF28pWtHi5jDvgrjCC6J8vaVI1V9gG4nnf5P__d_XcZLoExl_gwrek7bWPDZMjJjHeftgbWsWLgtd47bs6yUiUMdQYHrtOqUHmOg_BqtjD4hAhqJXp_c2WgDRycXb8-TSiqBRhP64MK1Rzg7ezU9Ygx6kuR3mlZKa6VO1hxcVOCsS8ZG00OwwiqMxFs9fG9UeMGUX3uzw7StMvuF-daE-RAwCBh4wYVy3n8Chuu9UcHW3meckQUMzoF1_bFLGLGG3P1Zdr-aHa23MPEek9XH25NypnY8CavmNdCbLvlfcz08_AQAAP__BBJ_3Q) operationally during the shutdown.
## Pfizer Prices Cut Under TrumpRx Plan
President Trump announced a deal with Pfizer to lower drug prices for Americans and launch a government-run site, [TrumpRx](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-upCAUxp8GOwweQMeCYhu72XZLg3LQs0ExwAw7-_Q33le43Zd8yffn9-c5Py0FOrff9kBTfJxPrDlgKZjmLb4xnQeeJe-v4mI9DwpUbJpBgOad4KKbnaXwmVXjjEcElA2abtCj7CT0utkNLL1eJcDolVv7xyA8LHrAVdnRj8PSN2TutE6IrhNiULLVToBW2kvl5AOsYkqgoxJT-zfSWXb04XMva9d4NMHspVyZyV8MJgZTrbVdl3y_uH0G0y0ZTJen_5h4iTxjCNyl15b5gY5WS46HWDHxK9GKmZf0Oq70j1dcMhVkMDXJuKtSCJT7gSmxHZbCd3_5EbS3ga8AAAD__3NMgzQ), which will sell discounted prescriptions starting in early 2026. Pfizer said the deal will cover most of its primary care drugs and select specialty brands, with [discounts averaging](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuO3DAQRE9DZRzwI608gYJZGMx2EwcOBX5aUtsUKZCtocenN-QrbNZAFwqv3s-P-cNixLR-2h0mWvKcoNUIRFDmNT-hpB0S1e2kkFvaMSLZMiuhBi4FF3IOFuNr7rswLQAKdAeTHIe7llq9Dd02eQfg71Y56eRde6m1l25QiwvWhVHqDqerTQohpRBjr29DEGroh0X3QX9Ttme9gICUy-1XxkQbLPF1kd183rs4bURHZfrBlGHKtNZue03XjykDiZ-VKXONYsocOSKhv862IQHf8lmBU-Y2pXwmD5zKuR_lDw_lXLk7X5hW3sDVK21T4AFs5A1p48eCf6EwZWzhj4f8fH_-Nkyb7DEw_f39cgqt_oBydGUKR8MYsb6NrBfrbjH-p6cvKX9O6l8AAAD__ynYmQc) 50% and reaching up to 85%. Separately, Trump said Pfizer would offer all of its drugs to Medicaid at a reduced, “most favored nation” price, as well as introduce any new drugs to the U.S. market at the reduced prices. As part of the deal, Pfizer will move [drug manufacturing](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGS4yAMhp8Gd2SEwHG2cHEN3V57pYcEEXOHIQPysrmnv_Fe3mA7zUj6fn2jX-_Lu4sp5vtPt9HMoSyZekvETHW5lw-qeaPMbd3Zl563mCK7uiDgKBVIUIt3MT0XM_g5ECHpgWY1jW9aaTyPwzqfwREEvDpt3CWctRkD0qSVUsaP11sY4nzQFIBSAJPRp9EDjmYM2nh9QWeEAfKRSz39LjHzSiE9j8tOt7INaV6ZH03oHwKtQNt7P-3NcfHueQwItI1LfQq0h5lAe6QJtPAm0GoQaB8h_qUque7bQ7qcy55vdFjLL4osWWbq__v1U3a6tsgk--pYxiYjv1br56t4ARuX2x_p634_Yi9noyfQE8Ak0A519o8eU4rtPAkD983F9CXE3_rCx4z_AgAA__9NtJzY) to the U.S. In exchange, the company gets a three-year grace period exempting it from Trump’s planned 100% tariffs on imports.
## List: Europe's Biggest Armies
Russia and Ukraine now have the [largest active military forces in Europe](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOtCytkNSuPgbuvztlcjEC-zJ2MjeBOWe_sTdI1w7Gs18-j7u491x4Lj8dxtZmdMY6SiBRCiPS3pRjhtFKetTfDrixoHF5REBTdNBA93oHYf3qGtvZyIkVZPtenNTncKLqVfrJu0JFBpwcLteZqMnjeQnNJPp1Q1qtudaB9B1AL1WrfGARptZaa-u6HSlgTxLyu1n4igrzeF9krWPtNXBriJ7qdS_CocKh-M42heXpwsPt7O4wEXOYoXDb8xf5Bt65rRTaSZeFirSuLwxlYZjc7JUONTZ-v3gELhc-krDsjkOP4fyJ0svi98BAAD__5l5fOA), with 1.3 million and 900,000 troops respectively, according to GlobalFirepower data for 2025. Together, it's estimated they outnumber all of NATO’s European armies combined. Among NATO members, Turkey (355,200 active), Poland (202,100), and the U.K. (184,860) top the list. By comparison, the U.S. ranks [among the largest globally](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ8GOpB_1gcpXKS57tKmRAteYCNjI9icdW8fXS5vkHpGn2a-z9twQ46clg_cyMuch0TljCRCx7DkOx1poyTn-i0hl7RxZMFjMMq4RqtG6SEgx8cAdfAzkSFbk9ede7PamourVz-BHhEcjjjaAJPpwfQTqLmnPnS9nWr2T5pWSmulOrCtC8o4cLOFYHuDUIGiwJKP9itzkpXm-Hgua6e81dGvIvtZ2ffKXCtzLaW0S8wjxpkP2nN59SpzxUn4Ts3fhUezYXrF-7rXhw974Rj5vHQVqGVDjr98-ZeUuzc_AQAA__9Mf3hR), with about 1,328,000 active personnel across its military branches. Countries like Finland and Estonia maintain large reserve forces despite smaller active rosters, underscoring how Europe’s military posture is shifting sharply in response to ongoing conflict. clean_body: The federal government shut down last night just before midnight as Democrats rejected a resolution to fund the government for seven more weeks unless it contained new money for health-care subsidies. | | | | | | Wednesday, October 1, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1940, the Pennsylvania Turnpike opened, an engineering marvel touted as . The new, mostly four-lane roadway immediately exceeded predictions for usage, with 2.4 million vehicles traveling it annually in its first years. In today's Flyover Podcast , we take a closer look at a growing religious revival among Gen Z Americans, who are increasingly embracing Christianity. You can . And don't forget: In addition to The Flyover Podcast , our team also publishes nine state-specific editions that offer straight and unbiased coverage of the most interesting news in these states: 🤠 , 🐊, 🍑, , 🛶, 🎸, 🏈, 🗻, and 🌵. You can sign up for free to any of them by clicking on its link. Tired of pop-ups, autoplay videos, and creepy ads tracking you online? Today's sponsor is that can block them all for a faster, safer, frustration-free browsing experience. | Federal Government Shuts Down The last night just after midnight as Democrats rejected a resolution to fund the government for seven more weeks because it didn't include money for health-care subsidies. The White House has said it would institute widespread layoffs and potentially take on government programs if the government shut down. President Trump can close functions he considers non-essential at agencies lacking funding. The military, federal law enforcement, the U.S. Post Office, and air traffic control will remain operational, and benefits such as Social Security and Medicare will also continue, though there may be disruptions and delays. In previous shutdowns, some federal services, including national parks, federal buildings and museums, were closed, but officials say parks may stay open this time. operationally during the shutdown. Pfizer Prices Cut Under TrumpRx Plan President Trump announced a deal with Pfizer to lower drug prices for Americans and launch a government-run site, , which will sell discounted prescriptions starting in early 2026. Pfizer said the deal will cover most of its primary care drugs and select specialty brands, with 50% and reaching up to 85%. Separately, Trump said Pfizer would offer all of its drugs to Medicaid at a reduced, “most favored nation” price, as well as introduce any new drugs to the U.S. market at the reduced prices. As part of the deal, Pfizer will move to the U.S. In exchange, the company gets a three-year grace period exempting it from Trump’s planned 100% tariffs on imports. List: Europe's Biggest Armies Russia and Ukraine now have the , with 1.3 million and 900,000 troops respectively, according to GlobalFirepower data for 2025. Together, it's estimated they outnumber all of NATO’s European armies combined. Among NATO members, Turkey (355,200 active), Poland (202,100), and the U.K. (184,860) top the list. By comparison, the U.S. ranks , with about 1,328,000 active personnel across its military branches. Countries like Finland and Estonia maintain large reserve forces despite smaller active rosters, underscoring how Europe’s military posture is shifting sharply in response to ongoing conflict. body:
We’re staring at an ENORMOUS fundraising gap

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-gL6fCfXAtnE50X6Hiw)Peter, I’ll shoot straight with you — we missed our Q3 fundraising goal.
Now we're staring at a $37 MILLION fundraising gap against the Democrats. They're already pouring that money into ads to buy back control of Congress and halt the Trump agenda.
And they’re not even hiding their plans: Chuck Schumer is using a government shutdown as a political weapon to undermine the America First agenda Americans voted for last November.
The Democrats couldn’t win at the ballot box, so now they’re turning to money and obstruction. That raises an age-old question: “Quis custodiet ipsos custodes?” — Who watches the watchers?
The answer is the American people, and the only way to hold them accountable is to close this gap and expand our House Majority.
That’s why I need your immediate help, Peter. [Can I count on you to kickstart our Q4 efforts with a $15 donation?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)[CLOSE THE GAP & DEFEND OUR MAJORITY
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Petty politics has no place in America. Donate now to fuel the fight for an America First House Majority and cement President Trump's agenda.

The Democrats couldn’t win at the ballot box, so now they’re turning to money and obstruction. That raises an age-old question: “Quis custodiet ipsos custodes?” — Who watches the watchers? subject: Donald Trump attacked me from the Oval Office

body:
There is nothing — absolutely nothing — that will divert my attention from the needs of Virginia families.
| | | | |

| | | | |

| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T108ce8f6-57e3-4d3c-94e8-38a021ed9159/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| Peter, Donald Trump attacked me from the Oval Office. But I will not be rattled. I will not be intimidated by somebody who's fired thousands of Virginians, driven up costs, and closed our rural health clinics. _In fact, let the President come down to Virginia and answer for the chaos he's unleashed._ There is nothing — absolutely nothing — that will divert my attention from the needs of Virginia families. [So, I hope I can earn your support today. Your contribution of $10 or more will be split with Blue Amp Action to help us stand up to Trump's attacks. This is the biggest election of 2025, and we need to raise more resources to reach voters with the truth.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdaba873e-a583-4a43-a457-1a3339912a74/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te40a30ef-e9ed-40ad-b1a8-325669602c4b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Georgia Momentum: Big Wins, Key Deadlines & How You Can Help
| | | | | [](https://fsdy8nbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Wrh-FUgAqIEq8Njup_hWLJguwnmXWY6zdUcSGbXhIELadw_uqDN9UqkQe11-31mWBtX9R92G-VulENX4n8mObThQQf2DAoDE1xn7qC2ogWGthquTDqtROEHIsvbseg4lb1VipAwalBM1DOFLhDoSPmHRtTfb0TmM-SNnxhjsj94=&c=bq8ML_1u1uk8sYUTH5Qa5ZM6Wg_-gRRFtntUwpYzcMXw4tm4sWyH0A==&ch=a1I8al5t7SSKmo8-aakit1Pw69IbA7eaOZZbMicA2NDyx2BJTPAAPQ==)
---
| |
---
| Greater Georgia Newsletter October 2025
---
| |
---
| Our movement continues to feel the loss of Charlie Kirk, a conservative leader who dedicated his life to empowering the next generation and defending our values. Charlie's influence stretched far beyond any single organization -- he inspired countless young people to step up, get involved, and never be afraid to speak the truth.
As we remember him, we look ahead with determination. Greater Georgia is committed to carrying his legacy forward: engaging more voters, building stronger families, and ensuring the conservative movement stays energized and bold.
---
| |
---
|
## National Voter Registration Day
---
On September 17th, Greater Georgia marked National Voter Registration Day by hosting a special phone banking night and the response was incredible. Phone banking is a great tool for registration because canvassing is more difficult when potential voters live miles apart.
Volunteers lit up the phones, helping us cross an exciting milestone: 15,000 calls and counting!
---
|
---
Use the link below to sign up to receive details about upcoming Greater Georgia volunteer opportunities, or contact Dylan Blitz at dylan@greatergeorgia.com.
---
| [HELP US REGISTER VOTERS](https://fsdy8nbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Wrh-FUgAqIEq8Njup_hWLJguwnmXWY6zdUcSGbXhIELadw_uqDN9UqkQe11-31mWBtX9R92G-VulENX4n8mObThQQf2DAoDE1xn7qC2ogWGthquTDqtROEHIsvbseg4lb1VipAwalBM1DOFLhDoSPl_-hcNJ9NhOFfw4aWthTIc=&c=bq8ML_1u1uk8sYUTH5Qa5ZM6Wg_-gRRFtntUwpYzcMXw4tm4sWyH0A==&ch=a1I8al5t7SSKmo8-aakit1Pw69IbA7eaOZZbMicA2NDyx2BJTPAAPQ==)
---
|
---
## On Campus: UGA for the Day
---
Our team was on the ground at the University of Georgia on September 13rd and connected with more than 100 students, registering new voters, and spreading the word about upcoming elections.
And we're not stopping there... Next stop: Kennesaw State!
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
|
## Conservative Victory in Senate District 21
---
| Congratulations to Senator-elect Jason Dickerson! Republicans scored a big win in this special election protecting the commonsense, conservative majority in the State Senate.
We're looking forward to building on this momentum in this November's statewide and special elections.
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
INSPIRING FAITH AND FREEDOM
---
| We enjoyed an inspiring evening with our friends at the Faith and Freedom Coalition and their Special Guest Kelly Loeffler.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---

subject: Bruce Bochy Out, Rocket Explodes, and Wemby Grows
body:
The Texas Rangers and manager Bruce Bochy have decided by “mutual agreement” that he will not coach the team next year after the 81-81 Rangers missed the playoffs for the second year in a row.
